The YAML parser module is organized into several sub-modules:\n//!\n//! - [`error`] - Comprehensive error types and error handling\n//! - [`types`] - Result types for parsing operations\n//! - [`parser`] - YAML-specific parser trait and implementations\n//! - [`syntax_validator`] - Syntax validation functionality\n//! - [`syntax_detector`] - Syntax error detection (indentation, delimiters, structure)\n//! - [`line_parser`] - Core data structures for representing parsed YAML lines\n//! - [`scope`] - Hierarchical scope representation for duplicate key detection\n//!\n//! ## Quick Start\n//!\n//! ```no_run\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::{parse_yaml, ParseResult};\n//!\n//! fn main() {\n//!     let yaml = r#\"\n//!         name: example\n//!         value: 42\n//!     \"#;\n//!\n//!     let result = parse_yaml(yaml);\n//!     if result.is_success() {\n//!         println!(\"Parsed successfully\");\n//!     }\n//! }\n//! ```\n//!\n//! ## Line Parser\n//!\n//! The [`line_parser`] module provides the foundational types for line-based YAML analysis:\n//!\n//! ```ignore\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::line_parser::{YamlLine, LineType};\n//!\n//! let line = YamlLine::new(1, \"key: value\", 0, LineType::MappingKey);\n//! assert_eq!(line.line_number(), 1);\n//! assert_eq!(line.raw_content(), \"key: value\");\n//! assert_eq!(line.line_type(), LineType::MappingKey);\n//! ```\n\npub mod error;\npub mod types;\npub mod parser;\npub mod syntax_validator;\npub mod syntax_detector;\npub mod line_parser;\npub mod scope;\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod syntax_detector_tests;\n\n// Re-export main types for convenience\npub use error::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind, Result};\npub use types::{\n    OperationResult, ParseMetadata, ParseResult, ParseWarning, ParseWarningKind,\n    ValidationResult, ValidationError, ValidationWarning, Status,\n    ErrorCode, ErrorType,\n};\npub use parser::Parser as YamlParser;\npub use syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator;\npub use syntax_detector::{SyntaxDetector, DelimiterErrorType, IndentationErrorType};\npub use line_parser::{\n    LineType, YamlLine, LineContent, LineParseResult,\n    MappingKeyInfo, detect_mapping_key, is_comment_line, strip_inline_comment,\n    classify_line_type, calculate_indentation,\n};\npub use scope::{\n    Scope, ScopeStack, DuplicateKeyError, KeyContext,\n    extract_key_context, get_leading_whitespace_length,\n};\n\n// Re-export comprehensive configuration from config module\npub use crate::parsers::config::{\n    ParserConfig, ParserMode, ParserConfigBuilder, TypeConstructor, ValidationHook,\n    ValidatorConfig, ValidationMode, ValidatorConfigBuilder,\n    default_parser_config, default_validator_config,\n};\n\n/// Version of the YAML parser module\npub const VERSION: &str = env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\");\n\n/// Create a new YAML parser with default configuration\n///\n/// This is a convenience function that creates a `BasicParser` with\n/// default (lenient) configuration.\npub fn new_parser() -> parser::BasicParser {\n    parser::BasicParser::new()\n}\n\n/// Create a new strict YAML parser\n///\n/// This is a convenience function that creates a `BasicParser` with\n/// strict configuration (rejects unknown fields, disallows duplicates).\npub fn new_strict_parser() -> parser::BasicParser {\n    parser::BasicParser::strict()\n}\n\n/// Parse YAML content from a string using default configuration\n///\n/// This is a convenience function for quick parsing operations.\npub fn parse_yaml(content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n    new_parser().parse_str(content)\n}\n\n/// Parse YAML content from a file using default configuration\n///\n/// This is a convenience function for quick file parsing operations.\npub fn parse_yaml_file(path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n    new_parser().parse_file(path)\n}\n","numLines":119,"startLine":1,"totalLines":119}}}

&ParserConfig;\n51\t\n52\t    /// Set the parser configuration\n53\t    ///\n54\t    /// # Arguments\n55\t    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n56\t    ///\n57\t    /// # Returns\n58\t    /// implementation\n65\t///\n66\t/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n67\t/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n68\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n69\tpub struct BasicParser {\n70\t    config: ParserConfig,\n71\t    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n72\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n73\t    /// Current line type being processed (key-bearing vs indent-only)\n74\t    current_line_type: LineClassification,\n75\t    /// Current indent transition state for tracking scope operations\n76\t    current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState,\n77\t    /// Current scope depth (number of active scopes in the hierarchy)\n78\t    scope_depth: usize,\n79\t}\n80\t\n81\timpl BasicParser {\n82\t    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n83\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n84\t        Self {\n85\t            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n86\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n87\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n88\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n89\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t\n93\t    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n94\t    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n95\t        Self {\n96\t            config,\n97\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n98\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n99\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n100\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n101\t        }\n102\t    }\n103\t\n104\t    /// Create a new strict parser\n105\t    ///\n106\t    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n107\t    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n108\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n109\t        Self {\n110\t            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n111\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n112\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n113\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n114\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n115\t        }\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Get the current line type being processed\n119\t    pub fn current_line_type(&self) -> LineClassification {\n120\t        self.current_line_type\n121\t    }\n122\t\n123\t    /// Check if current line is key-bearing\n124\t    pub fn is_key_bearing_line(&self) -> bool {\n125\t        self.current_line_type.is_key_bearing()\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    /// Check if current line is indent-only\n129\t    pub fn is_indent_only_line(&self) -> bool {\n130\t        self.current_line_type.is_indent_only()\n131\t    }\n132\t\n133\t    /// Check if current line is empty\n134\t    pub fn is_empty_line(&self) -> bool {\n135\t        self.current_line_type.is_empty()\n136\t    }\n137\t\n138\t    /// Get the current indent transition state\n139\t    pub fn current_transition_state(&self) -> &IndentTransitionState {\n140\t        &self.current_transition_state\n141\t    }\n142\t\n143\t    /// Check if the parser is currently entering a scope\n144\t    pub fn is_entering_scope(&self) -> bool {\n145\t        self.current_transition_state.is_entering_scope()\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    /// Check if the parser is currently exiting a scope\n149\t    pub fn is_exiting_scope(&self) -> bool {\n150\t        self.current_transition_state.is_exiting_scope()\n151\t    }\n152\t\n153\t    /// Check if the parser is at the same scope level\n154\t    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n155\t        self.current_transition_state.is_same_level()\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    /// Get the current scope depth\n159\t    ///\n160\t    /// Returns the number of active scopes in the hierarchy.\n161\t    /// Root scope has depth 1.\n162\t    pub fn scope_depth(&self) -> usize {\n163\t        self.scope_depth\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    /// Check if we're at the root scope (depth == 1)\n167\t    ///\n168\t    /// # Returns\n169\t    ///\n170\t    /// `true` if we're at the root scope, `false` otherwise\n171\t    pub fn is_at_root(&self) -> bool {\n172\t        self.scope_depth == 1\n173\t    }\n174\t\n175\t    /// Check if we're in a nested scope (depth > 1)\n176\t    ///\n177\t    /// # Returns\n178\t    ///\n179\t    /// `true` if we're in a nested scope, `false` if at root\n180\t    pub fn is_in_nested_scope(&self) -> bool {\n181\t        self.scope_depth > 1\n182\t    }\n183\t\n184\t    /// Get a reference to the scope stack\n185\t    pub fn scope_stack(&self) -> &ScopeStack {\n186\t        &self.scope_stack\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    /// Get mutable reference to the scope stack\n190\t    pub fn scope_stack_mut(&mut self) -> &mut ScopeStack {\n191\t        &mut self.scope_stack\n192\t    }\n193\t\n194\t    /// Get the current scope from the stack\n195\t    pub fn current_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n196\t        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() {\n197\t            None\n198\t        } else {\n199\t            self.scope_stack.scopes.last()\n200\t        }\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    /// Get parent scope at a specific depth\n204\t    ///\n205\t    /// # Arguments\n206\t    ///\n207\t    /// * `depth_offset` - How many levels up to go (1 = immediate parent, 2 = grandparent, etc.)\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// # Returns\n210\t    ///\n211\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a parent exists at that level, `None` otherwise\n212\t    ///\n213\t    /// # Examples\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// ```\n216\t    /// let parser = BasicParser::new();\n217\t    /// // If in scope \"a.b.c\", depth_offset=1 returns \"b\" scope, depth_offset=2 returns \"a\" scope\n218\t    /// ```\n219\t    pub fn parent_scope(&self, depth_offset: usize) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n220\t        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() || depth_offset == 0 {\n221\t            None\n222\t        } else if depth_offset >= self.scope_stack.scopes.len() {\n223\t            None\n224\t        } else {\n225\t            self.scope_stack.scopes.get(self.scope_stack.scopes.len() - 1 - depth_offset)\n226\t        }\n227\t    }\n228\t\n229\t    /// Get the immediate parent scope (one level up)\n230\t    pub fn immediate_parent_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n231\t        self.parent_scope(1)\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Get the scope hierarchy path as a string\n235\t    ///\n236\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n237\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n238\t    pub fn scope_path(&self) -> String {\n239\t        self.scope_stack.get_scope_path()\n240\t    }\n241\t\n242\t    /// Get all scopes in the hierarchy as a slice\n243\t    pub fn scope_hierarchy(&self) -> &[crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope] {\n244\t        &self.scope_stack.scopes\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    /// Update scope depth to match the current scope stack state\n248\t    ///\n249\t    /// This should be called after operations that modify the scope stack\n250\t    /// to keep the scope_depth field in sync.\n251\t    fn update_scope_depth(&mut self) {\n252\t        self.scope_depth = self.scope_stack.depth();\n253\t    }\n254\t\n255\t    /// Get all indent transitions from parsing\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// This requires re-parsing to collect transitions. For more efficient\n258\t    /// access, use the scope stack directly during validation.\n259\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications and transition tracking.\n260\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<crate::parsers::yaml::scope::IndentTransition> {\n261\t        let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n262\t\n263\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n264\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n265\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n266\t\n267\t            // Skip document markers and directives\n268\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n269\t                // Update state to track that we're on an empty line (marker line)\n270\t                self.current_line_type = LineClassification::Empty;\n271\t                continue;\n272\t            }\n273\t\n274\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n275\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n276\t\n277\t            // Track line type in parser state\n278\t            self.current_line_type = line_type;\n279\t\n280\t            // Only track actual indent changes\n281\t            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n282\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n283\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n284\t\n285\t                // Update transition state to track this indent change\n286\t                let from_indent = scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n287\t                self.current_transition_state.update(from_indent, indent, has_key);\n288\t            }\n289\t        }\n290\t\n291\t        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    /// Get transition counts by type\n295\t    ///\n296\t    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n297\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications during counting.\n298\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&mut self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n299\t        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n300\t\n301\t        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n302\t        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n303\t        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n304\t\n305\t        (enter, exit, same)\n306\t    }\n307\t\n308\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n311\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n312\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n313\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n314\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n315\t\n316\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n317\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n318\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n319\t\n320\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n321\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n322\t\n323\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n324\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n325\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n326\t                {\n327\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n328\t                }\n329\t                scope_stack.reset();\n330\t                continue;\n331\t            }\n332\t\n333\t            // Skip YAML directives\n334\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n335\t                continue;\n336\t            }\n337\t\n338\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n339\t\n340\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n341\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n342\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n343\t            if indent_changed {\n344\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n345\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n346\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n347\t            }\n348\t\n349\t            // Handle blank lines, comments, and indent-only lines with consistent logic\n350\t            // These lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n351\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n352\t            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n353\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n354\t                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n355\t                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n356\t                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n357\t                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n358\t                }\n359\t                continue;\n360\t            }\n361\t\n362\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n363\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but DO trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n364\t            // This ensures proper scope cleanup when returning to outer levels through blank lines\n365\t            // CRITICAL: Scope stack must exit on indent decrease even without keys to maintain consistency\n366\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n367\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n368\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n369\t                    // Record the indent transition with has_key=false for indent-only lines\n370\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n371\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n372\t                } else if indent > scope_stack.current_indent() {\n373\t                    // Indent increased on indent-only line - just record the transition, don't enter scope\n374\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n375\t                }\n376\t                continue;\n377\t            }\n378\t\n379\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n380\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n381\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n382\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n383\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n384\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n385\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n386\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n387\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n388\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n389\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n390\t                                    dup_err.message()\n391\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n392\t                            }\n393\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n394\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n395\t                            {\n396\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n397\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n398\t                            }\n399\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n400\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n401\t                                line_num_1index,\n402\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n403\t                            );\n404\t                        } else {\n405\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n406\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n407\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n408\t                            {\n409\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n410\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n411\t                            }\n412\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n413\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n414\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n415\t                                    dup_err.message()\n416\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n417\t                            }\n418\t                        }\n419\t                    } else {\n420\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n421\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n422\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n423\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n424\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n425\t                        {\n426\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n427\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n428\t                        }\n429\t                    }\n430\t                }\n431\t                Ordering::Less => {\n432\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n433\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n434\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n435\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n436\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n437\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n438\t\n439\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n440\t                    {\n441\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n442\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n443\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n444\t                    }\n445\t\n446\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n447\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n448\t                    }\n449\t\n450\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n451\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n452\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n453\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n454\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n455\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n456\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n457\t                                    dup_err.message()\n458\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n459\t                            }\n460\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n461\t                            {\n462\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n463\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n464\t                            }\n465\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n466\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n467\t                                line_num_1index,\n468\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n469\t                            );\n470\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n471\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n472\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n473\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n474\t                                    dup_err.message()\n475\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n476\t                            }\n477\t                        }\n478\t                    }\n479\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n480\t                }\n481\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n482\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n483\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n484\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n485\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n486\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n487\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n488\t                            {\n489\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n490\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n491\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n492\t                            }\n493\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n494\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n495\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n496\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n497\t                                    dup_err.message()\n498\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n499\t                            }\n500\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n501\t                            {\n502\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n503\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n504\t                            }\n505\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n506\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n507\t                                line_num_1index,\n508\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n509\t                            );\n510\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n511\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n512\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n513\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n514\t                                    dup_err.message()\n515\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n516\t                            }\n517\t                        }\n518\t                    }\n519\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n520\t                }\n521\t            }\n522\t\n523\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n524\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n525\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n526\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n527\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n528\t                    {\n529\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n530\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n531\t                    }\n532\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n533\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n534\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n535\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n536\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n537\t                            dup_err.message()\n538\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n539\t                    }\n540\t                } else {\n541\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n542\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n543\t                    {\n544\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n545\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n546\t                    }\n547\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n548\t                }\n549\t            }\n550\t        }\n551\t\n552\t        duplicate_errors\n553\t    }\n554\t}\n555\t\n556\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n557\t    fn default() -> Self {\n558\t        Self::new()\n559\t    }\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n563\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n564\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n565\t        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n566\t        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n567\t\n568\t        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n569\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n570\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n571\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n572\t\n573\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n574\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n575\t\n576\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n577\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n578\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n579\t                {\n580\t                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n581\t                }\n582\t                scope_stack.reset();\n583\t                continue;\n584\t            }\n585\t\n586\t            // Skip YAML directives\n587\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n588\t                continue;\n589\t            }\n590\t\n591\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n592\t\n593\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n594\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n595\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n596\t            if indent_changed {\n597\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n598\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n599\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n600\t            }\n601\t\n602\t            // Handle blank lines, comments, and indent-only lines with consistent logic\n603\t            // These lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n604\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n605\t            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n606\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n607\t                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n608\t                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n609\t                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n610\t                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n611\t                }\n612\t                continue;\n613\t            }\n614\t\n615\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n616\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but DO trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n617\t            // This ensures proper scope cleanup when returning to outer levels through blank lines\n618\t            // CRITICAL: Scope stack must exit on indent decrease even without keys to maintain consistency\n619\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n620\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n621\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n622\t                    // Record the indent transition with has_key=false for indent-only lines\n623\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n624\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n625\t                } else if indent > scope_stack.current_indent() {\n626\t                    // Indent increased on indent-only line - just record the transition, don't enter scope\n627\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, false, line);\n628\t                }\n629\t                continue;\n630\t            }\n631\t\n632\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n633\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n634\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n635\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n636\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n637\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n638\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n639\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n640\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n641\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n642\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n643\t                                    dup_err.message()\n644\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n645\t                            }\n646\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n647\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n648\t                            {\n649\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n650\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n651\t                            }\n652\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n653\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n654\t                                line_num_1index,\n655\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n656\t                            );\n657\t                        } else {\n658\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n659\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n660\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n661\t                            {\n662\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n663\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n664\t                            }\n665\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n666\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n667\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n668\t                                    dup_err.message()\n669\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n670\t                            }\n671\t                        }\n672\t                    } else {\n673\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n674\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n675\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n676\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n677\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n678\t                        {\n679\t                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n680\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n681\t                        }\n682\t                    }\n683\t                }\n684\t                Ordering::Less => {\n685\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n686\t                    // Record the indent transition with key\n687\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, true, line);\n688\t\n689\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n690\t                    {\n691\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n692\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n693\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n694\t                    }\n695\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n696\t\n697\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n698\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n699\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n700\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n701\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n702\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n703\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n704\t                                    dup_err.message()\n705\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n706\t                            }\n707\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n708\t                            {\n709\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n710\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n711\t                            }\n712\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n713\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n714\t                                line_num_1index,\n715\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n716\t                            );\n717\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n718\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n719\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n720\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n721\t                                    dup_err.message()\n722\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n723\t                            }\n724\t                        }\n725\t                    }\n726\t                }\n727\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n728\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n729\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n730\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n731\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n732\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n733\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n734\t                            {\n735\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n736\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n737\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n738\t                            }\n739\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n740\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n741\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n742\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n743\t                                    dup_err.message()\n744\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n745\t                            }\n746\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n747\t                            {\n748\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n749\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n750\t                            }\n751\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n752\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n753\t                                line_num_1index,\n754\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n755\t                            );\n756\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n757\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n758\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n759\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n760\t                                    dup_err.message()\n761\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n762\t                            }\n763\t                        }\n764\t                    }\n765\t                }\n766\t            }\n767\t\n768\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n769\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n770\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n771\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n772\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n773\t                    {\n774\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n775\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n776\t                    }\n777\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n778\t                }\n779\t\n780\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n781\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n782\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n783\t                    {\n784\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n785\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n786\t                    }\n787\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n788\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n789\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n790\t                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n791\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n792\t                            dup_err.message()\n793\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n794\t                    }\n795\t                } else {\n796\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n797\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n798\t                    {\n799\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n800\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n801\t                    }\n802\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n803\t                }\n804\t            }\n805\t        }\n806\t\n807\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n808\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n809\t            Ok(value) => {\n810\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n811\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n812\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n813\t            }\n814\t            Err(err) => {\n815\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n816\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n817\t                    err.to_string()\n818\t                } else {\n819\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n820\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n821\t                };\n822\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n823\t            }\n824\t        }\n825\t    }\n826\t\n827\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n828\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n829\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n830\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n831\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n832\t        }\n833\t    }\n834\t\n835\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n836\t        // Read file content\n837\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n838\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n839\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n840\t        }\n841\t    }\n842\t\n843\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n844\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n845\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n846\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n847\t        } else {\n848\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n849\t        };\n850\t\n851\t        // Run syntax validation\n852\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n853\t\n854\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n855\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n856\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n857\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n858\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n859\t                result.valid = false;\n860\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n861\t            }\n862\t        }\n863\t\n864\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n865\t        if result.is_valid() {\n866\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n867\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n868\t\n869\t            // Merge errors from detector\n870\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n871\t                result.valid = false;\n872\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n873\t            }\n874\t        }\n875\t\n876\t        result\n877\t    }\n878\t\n879\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n880\t        // Read file content\n881\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n882\t            Ok(content) => content,\n883\t            Err(err) => {\n884\t                return ValidationResult {\n885\t                    valid: false,\n886\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n887\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n888\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n889\t                    )],\n890\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n891\t                };\n892\t            }\n893\t        };\n894\t\n895\t        // Validate the content\n896\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n897\t    }\n898\t\n899\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n900\t        &self.config\n901\t    }\n902\t\n903\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n904\t    where\n905\t        Self: Sized,\n906\t    {\n907\t        Self {\n908\t            config,\n909\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n910\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n911\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n912\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n913\t        }\n914\t    }\n915\t}\n916\t\n917\t// ============================================================================\n918\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n919\t// ============================================================================\n920\t\n921\t#[cfg(test)]\n922\tmod integration_tests {\n923\t    use super::*;\n924\t\n925\t    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n926\t    #[test]\n927\t    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n928\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n929\t        let yaml = r#\"\n930\tservices:\n931\t  web:\n932\t    host: localhost\n933\t    port: 8080\n934\t  database:\n935\t    host: db.example.com\n936\t    port: 5432\n937\t\"#;\n938\t\n939\t        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n940\t        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n941\t\n942\t        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n943\t        let value = result.unwrap();\n944\t        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n945\t        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n946\t    }\n947\t\n948\t    /// Test parse_str with deeply nested YAML\n949\t    #[test]\n950\t    fn has 'host' and 'port' in different scopes\n978\t        let yaml = r#\"\n979\tservices:\n980\t  web:\n981\t    host: localhost\n982\t    port: 8080\n983\t  database:\n984\t    host: {\n1004\t        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // Strict parser doesn't allow duplicates\n1005\t\n1006\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1007\tconfig:\n1008\t  server:\n1009\t    host: localhost\n1010\t    host: duplicate-value\n1011\t    port: 8080\n1012\t\"#;\n1013\t\n1014\t        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n1015\t #[test]\n1051\t    fn test_parse_str_mixed_mapping_sequence() {\n1052\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n1053\t\n1054\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1055\tservices:\n1056\t  web:\n1057\t #[test]\n1078\t    fn test_parse_str_multiple_indent_transitions() {\n1079\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n1080\t\n1081\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1082\tlevel1_a:\n1083\t  level2_a:\n1084\t    level3:\n1085\t      value: 1\n1086\t  level2_b:\n1087\t    level3:\n1088\t      value: 2\n1089\tlevel1_b:\n1090\t  level2:\n1091\t    level3:\n1092\t      value: 3\n1093\t\"#;\n1094\t\n1095\t BasicParser::new();\n1122\t\n1123\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1124\t# Configuration file\n1125\tname: test\n1126\t\n1127\t# Server settings\n1128\tserver:\n1129\t  host: localhost\n1130\t  # Port keys\n1144\t    #[test]\n1145\t    fn test_validate_str_duplicate_detection() {\n1146\t        let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n1147\t\n1148\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1149\tconfig:\n1150\t r#\"\n1680\troot:\n1681\t  level1:\n1682\t    level2: value\n1683\t  level1_sibling: value2\n1684\t\"#;\n1685\t\n1686\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1687\t\n1688\t        // Should have transitions for indent changes\n1689\t        assert!(!transitions.is_empty(), \"Should track indent transitions\");\n1690\t\n1691\t        // Verify transitions are properly classified\n1692\t        let enter_transitions: Vec<_> = transitions.iter()\n1693\t            .filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope())\n1694\t            .collect();\n1695\t\n1696\t        let exit_transitions: Vec<_> = transitions.iter()\n1697\t            .filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope())\n1698\t            .collect();\n1699\t\n1700\t        // Should have both enter and exit transitions\");\n1703\t    }\n1704\t\n1705\t    /// Test transition type classification\n1706\t    #[test]\n1707\t    fn test_transition_type_classification() {\n1708\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1709\t\n1710\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1711\tkey1: value1\n1712\t  nested:\n1713\t    deep: value\n1714\tkey2: value2\n1715\t\"#;\n1716\t\n1717\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1718\t\n1719\t        // Check that all transitions are properly classified\n1720\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1721\t            let transition_type = transition.transition_type();\n1722\t\n1723\t            // Verify classification matches indent change\n1724\t            if transition.to_indent > transition.from_indent {\n1725\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope,\n1726\t                           \"Indent increase should be classified as EnterScope\");\n1727\t            } else if transition.to_indent < transition.from_indent {\n1728\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope,\n1729\t                           \"Indent decrease should be classified as ExitScope\");\n1730\t            } else {\n1731\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n1732\t                           \"No indent change should be classified as SameLevel\");\n1733\t            }\n1734\t        }\n1735\t    }\n1736\t\n1737\t    /// Test scope operation mapping\n1738\t    #[test]\n1739\t    fn test_scope_operation_mapping() {\n1740\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1741\t\n1742\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1743\tparent:\n1744\t  child: value\n1745\tsibling: value2\n1746\t\"#;\n1747\t\n1748\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1749\t\n1750\t        // Verify each transition has a valid scope operation\n1751\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1752\t            let operation = transition.scope_operation();\n1753\t\n1754\t            match operation {\n1755\t                \"enter-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_enter_scope()),\n1756\t                \"exit-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_exit_scope()),\n1757\t                \"stay-in-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_same_level()),\n1758\t                _ => panic!(\"Invalid scope operation: {}\", operation),\n1759\t            }\n1760\t        }\n1761\t    }\n1762\t\n1763\t    /// Test transition counts by type\n1764\t    #[test]\n1765\t    fn test_transition_counts() {\n1766\t value\n1775\t\"#;\n1776\t\n1777\t        let (enter, exit, same) = parser.get_transition_counts(yaml);\n1778\t\n1779\t        // Should have multiple transitions\n1780\t        assert!(enter > 0, \"Should have enter-scope transitions\");\n1781\t        assert!(exit > 0, \"Should have exit-scope transitions\");\n1782\t\n1783\t        // Enter and exit should be roughly balanced (with small difference due to starting at root)\n1784\t        assert!((enter as i32 - exit as i32).abs() <= 1,\n1785\t               \"Enter and exit counts should be balanced\");\n1786\t    }\n1787\t\n1788\t
